signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.4386 (implied) — NZD dominates G8 performance while CAD lags significantly. The 1.497% spread reflects a sharp divergence between the RBNZ hike bias and BoC hold stance. NZD COT LF net short -16,833 (23% long) vs RBNZ hike bias creates a high-conviction short-squeeze environment as NZD outperforms peers. CAD remains pressured by falling oil prices (WTI -4.15%) and vs USD.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Technical Support + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7767 — price breaks below key support as sellers take control in North American trade. Retail sentiment is at an extreme 85% long, directly diverging from COT LF net short -6,117 (33.6% long). This institutional vs retail divergence suggests further downside as retail longs are forced to liquidate into the break. 375bp USD carry advantage is failing to support the pair as safe-haven CHF demand accelerates.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Carry + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7259 — RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp carry premium supporting the 1W +0.81% gain. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) shows heavy institutional backing, while retail is at an extreme 90% short. This extreme divergence indicates retail is unsuccessfully fading the trend; a continuation higher is likely as retail capitulation provides further liquidity for the move. Watch for a break above 0.7300 to confirm.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry Headwind
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1780 — correlation vs EuroStoxx has shifted to +0.728 (z=+1.84σ), a significant break from the -0.130 norm. EUR is decoupling from domestic equity weakness, likely driven by DXY softening (97.84). However, the Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% spread maintains a 175bp carry disadvantage for the Euro, structurally limiting upside potential despite the recent 1M +2.35% rally. Watch for ECB meeting on 11 Jun for any shift in hold bias.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.297 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage keeps the pair supported despite recent 1M -2.18% retracement. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains crowded, leaving the pair vulnerable to rapid unwinds if VIX (17.1) spikes above 25. BoJ hike bias (16 Jun) acts as a secondary floor for JPY. Carry remains the dominant driver while volatility is normal. Watch 160.00 for renewed intervention rhetoric.
